title: organization — subprocessors
description: The organization module does not engage third-party subprocessors. All reads + writes hit the platform's own Postgres + Redis. Organization data never leaves the platform for organizational operations. This file documents the empty surface so compliance reviewers can quickly verify.
notes: Data never leaves the platform for Organization operations. Names, slugs, tree_path values, contact information, and branding assets stay on the platform's owned infrastructure.

The `logo_url` field references an asset URL — the asset itself lives on `storage::File` (which may in turn be backed by an object-store subprocessor). Any subprocessor obligation there is documented in the storage module's subprocessors.json, not here.
The tenant `Branding` model (owned by `branding`) may reference brand assets under CDN — those CDN subprocessor obligations live in the branding module.

Downstream notifications (OrganizationCreatedNotification, etc.) route through the notifications module which engages email/SMS subprocessors documented in notifications/subprocessors.json. Organization itself does not engage them directly.
